step.title: Load architecture and identify attack surfaces
step.action: Read architecture document and identify exposed endpoints, data flows, and trust boundaries.
If a test plan exists, load it for security-specific test requirements.

step.title: Run dependency vulnerability scan
step.action: Run the appropriate dependency scanner: npm audit, pip-audit, or govulncheck.
Capture all reported vulnerabilities with advisory IDs and affected versions.

step.title: Check OWASP Top 10 categories
step.action: Review codebase patterns against each OWASP Top 10 category.
Check for injection, broken auth, sensitive data exposure, XXE, broken access control, misconfiguration, XSS, insecure deserialization, known vulnerabilities, and insufficient logging.

step.title: Classify findings by severity
step.action: Classify each finding as critical, high, medium, or low severity.
Assess exploitability and blast radius for each finding.

step.title: Create remediation plan
step.action: Create a remediation plan prioritized by severity and exploitability.
Include specific fix guidance, estimated effort, and responsible owner for each finding.

steps.step: <template-output file="docs/testing-artifacts/security-test-report-.md"> Apply the template from template.md, filling all sections with security findings.
